description_long: This is a twin-engine, high-wing turboprop airplane with a predominantly blue and white livery, accented by red and orange stripes along the fuselage. The aircraft features a straight, high-mounted wing configuration with two propeller engines mounted on the wings. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is tricycle-type, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear visible beneath the cockpit. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage and is enclosed with multiple windows for visibility. The airplane bears airline branding and registration markings on the tail and wings.
description: A blue and white twin-engine, high-wing turboprop airplane with a conventional tail and tricycle landing gear, featuring airline branding and propeller engines on the wings.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the nose, cockpit windows, propeller engines, and high-mounted wings.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and trailing edge of the wings.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the tail, horizontal stabilizers, and the right-side propeller engine.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the cockpit, nose, and both propeller engines with visible landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all fixed and visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
